title: EIP-8163: Reserve EXTENSION (0xae) opcode
description: Reserves opcode 0xae as EXTENSION, guaranteed to never become a valid instruction on Ethereum L1 EVM. On L1 it behaves identically to INVALID (0xfe), causing an exceptional halt. Non-L1 EVM chains may use it as a prefix to encode custom extension instructions, enabling experimentation without risking opcode collisions with mainnet.
author: Bruce Collie (@Baltoli), Piotr Dobaczewski (@pdobacz)
laymanDescription: Sets aside one EVM instruction slot that Ethereum mainnet promises never to use. Other EVM-compatible chains and Layer 2s can then safely build custom features using that slot, knowing it will never conflict with future Ethereum upgrades.

endUsers.description: No impact. L1 behavior is unchanged; the reserved opcode already causes a halt today.

appDevs.description: No impact on L1 smart contract development. Developers on non-L1 EVMs gain a safe namespace for custom instructions.

walletDevs.description: No impact. The opcode reservation does not alter transaction processing or wallet interfaces.

toolingInfra.description: Debuggers and tracers may optionally label 0xae as EXTENSION instead of INVALID for clarity, but no functional changes are required.

layer2s.description: L2 EVM implementations gain a reserved opcode prefix for safe experimentation with custom instructions without risking L1 opcode collisions.

stakersNodes.description: No impact. Consensus and validator behavior are unaffected by this opcode reservation.

clClients.description: No changes required. This EIP concerns EVM opcode semantics only and has no consensus-layer implications.

elClients.description: No consensus-level changes required. Clients may optionally update trace output to label 0xae as EXTENSION rather than INVALID.

benefits: Enables non-L1 EVM chains to innovate without risking opcode collisions with Ethereum mainnet.
Successful extensions proven on L2s or sidechains can later be back-ported to L1 via separate EIPs.
Requires zero changes to Ethereum L1 execution client consensus logic.
Strengthens EVM ecosystem network effects by encouraging coordinated experimentation across chains.
tradeoffs: Permanently consumes one opcode slot on L1, reducing the pool of available opcodes for future L1 features.
